page: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 32 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a base speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared allocation model. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 10%, alongside strong multi-core CPU performance in the top 25%. Passmark CPU physics tests also register in the top 25%. However, memory bandwidth for both read and write operations falls within the average range. Single-threaded gzip compression performance is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database operations perform at average levels.

The shared CPU allocation model offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated, continuous CPU execution. The high memory-to-vCPU density makes this server appropriate for memory-optimized tasks, database hosting, and caching layers like Redis, where the average database operation speeds and static web serving throughput are sufficient. It is also suitable for moderate multi-threaded processing and medium-sized language model inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-m-32c-256gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor, offering 32 shared vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 256.0 GB of system memory, and 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, though its memory bandwidth and single-threaded gzip compression remain average to weak. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads that do not demand dedicated compute cores. This hardware profile is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized shared instance featuring AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for data-intensive workloads.
